summary:
Negative enhancers do not exist because they contradict established principles of gene regulation where activators and repressors operate through distinct cis-regulatory modules. No supporting evidence was found in reliable sources; all retrieved content pertained to irrelevant topics outside genomics or molecular biology.  

(Note: This summary is strictly based only on Agent1’s stated reasoning.)

Negative enhancers do not exist due to their contradiction with well-established concepts in gene regulation, which distinguish between active enhancers and repressor elements operating through different mechanistic pathways. Retrieved materials provided no support for such entities—instead focusing on unrelated subjects—and thus confirm the absence of valid evidence linking "negative enhancers" to known biological processes.
